text:
The town cat, Whiskers, always liked to chase fireflies at night. Tonight was no different. He darted through the twilight, leaving behind trails of light. Suddenly, a branch snapped under his weight. Startled, his pursuit was broken.

A sudden rustle in the bushes stopped Whiskers abruptly. A small grey mouse glanced at him curiously. Before hesitation could set in, Whiskers leaped forward, catching the mouse in his paw.

"Meow! There isn't a thing like you!" The mouse chirped.

"Well," began Whiskers, pondering. "Perhaps I've never had one before."

After an amused silence, the mouse spoke. "You wouldn't eat me?"

"No, why?"

"Just seem lonely."

"Who might I meet who needs a friend?"

"The squirrels. They say the nut-crackers are quite bright."

"That sounds intriguing. Maybe I'll join them sometime."

The mouse scampered away into the dusk, leaving
